codeReview.summary: Core publish wiring (modal validation, kind-10000 event creation, relay fan-out, monotonic created_at) is present, but the feature misses the required persistent 'last publish' indicator/timestamp on Settings after success.

issues.description: VAL-BACKUP-005 and VAL-BACKUP-031 require the Settings surface to retain a persistent 'most recent publish' indicator/timestamp (and reflect the race winner). `publishProfileBackup` returns `{ event, reached }` but does not persist any publish metadata to app state or IndexedDB, and the Settings UI only renders a static action row. After closing the modal or revisiting Settings, there is no persisted publish timestamp/indicator to display.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-screen-worker procedure requires agent-browser verification for changed UI and a RED-first failing test run capture, but this feature handoff still marks `followedProcedure: true` without either artifact. Skill procedure and enforcement appear out of sync.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton, handoff, skill file, and commit 05f2462799ba6540ab041151a4bc6f6d19cdc9c5. Found one blocking gap: publish success metadata is not persisted/displayed on Settings as required by VAL-BACKUP-005/031, so the feature does not fully satisfy the contract.
